pattern: A fixed-size ring buffer reuses freed slots by wrapping head and tail indices modulo the capacity, giving constant-time FIFO enqueue and dequeue without shifting elements.
hints: You only need a fixed array of size k plus a couple of integers; nothing has to move when you enqueue or dequeue.
Track where the queue starts and how many items it holds; the end position is derivable from those two.

Store a buffer, a head index, and a count. enQueue writes at (head + count) % k when not full; deQueue advances head modulo k and decrements count; Front and Rear read the appropriate slots or return -1 when empty.

edge_inputs.description: fill the queue, overflow, peek rear, then dequeue and reuse the freed slot
capacity one toggles between empty and full on each operation
Front and Rear on an empty queue return -1, and deQueue on empty fails
wrap-around: many enqueue/dequeue cycles must keep head and tail consistent past the array end

stress.description: long randomized mix of all operations against a small capacity
stress.note: small capacity forces frequent full/empty boundaries and wrap-around
